metadata.title: T-Mobile Ericsson RAN Causal DAG v2.0 — Complete Alarm-to-KPI Impact & Mitigation Graph
metadata.author: Sasanka Valluri
metadata.enhancement_notes: Added missing causal chains, cross-domain links, T4→T5 mappings, and playbooks for RSSI Hot/Cold, Transport, RU Fault, DAS Amplifier, License/Feature issues

node_tiers.T1: Physical/Alarm Layer — Hardware faults, physical measurements, DAS issues
node_tiers.T2: RF Observable Layer — UE-measured RF parameters (Samsung service mode)
node_tiers.T3: Parameter/Configuration Layer — Ericsson MO parameters from PAG scripts
node_tiers.T4: Protocol/Counter Layer — Ericsson PM counters from ENM/ENIQ
node_tiers.T5: KPI Layer — ACS Venue and MS2 Macro KPIs with exact thresholds

metadata.enhancement_notes_v3: v3.0 — Enhanced with LAYH023A IX package: 3,752 deployed parameters, CIQ cell configs, PCI plan, hardware bindings, integration sequence chain. Date: 2026-03-20

changes: Added LAH0050 venue deployment (3 sites, 9 gNBs, 76 NR cells, 24 LTE cells)
Added mmWave T1 validations (7 nodes: VSWR, fiber_loss, RRU_alarm, RSSI_hot/cold, baseband, sector_equip)
Added mmWave T3 defaults (8 nodes: EPS_fallback, A5_pcell/pscell, B1, QOS_5QI, PRACH, coverage, ANR_SON)
Added CDC_MMWAVE_VENUE cross-domain chain (7-step mmWave causality)
Generated 14 mmWave training pairs across 5 categories

enhancement_history.description: NR-DC (NR Dual Connectivity) comprehensive enhancement for Dodger Stadium mmWave venue
changes: Added 7 T3 NR-DC parameter nodes (nrDcAllowed, scgFailureHandling, CPC, bandCombo, Xn, NRCellRelation, xnIpAddrViaNgActive)
Added 5 T4 NR-DC counter nodes with REAL Ericsson PM counter names (pmNrdcSetup*, pmNrdcRel*, pmScgFailure*, pmXnSetup*, pmCpc*)
Added 4 T5 NR-DC KPIs (NR-DC Setup SR, SCG Failure Rate, Abnormal Release Rate, CPC SR)
Added 16 causal edges connecting NR-DC nodes across tiers
Added 3 NR-DC playbooks (Setup Failure, SCG Recovery, mmWave Venue NR-DC)
Added 3 NR-DC cross-domain chains (Setup Failure, Beam→SCG Drop, Silent Block)
Generated 28 NR-DC training pairs across 5 categories

enhancement_history.description: SN Change immediate collapse failure mode — from real protocol trace at Dodger Stadium
changes: Added 4 T3 nodes: tSCGconfigTimer, SN Change A3/A5 gap, beamSweepTimer/SSB, PDCP SN Change forwarding
Added 1 T4 node: SCG Change failure counters with pmNrScgFailureCause
Added T5 KPI: K_SN_CHANGE_SR mapped to NTM NR RET SA NRDC SN SN Total Drop Rate
Added 7 causal edges for SN Change failure paths
Added CDC_SN_CHANGE_COLLAPSE chain with full protocol trace evidence
Added PB_SN_CHANGE_COLLAPSE playbook with 7-step diagnostic procedure
enhancement_history.key_finding: NR-DC retainability failure is SN Change collapse, not SN Addition failure. New SN beam fails 14ms after setup. Root causes: tSCGconfigTimer, marginal PSCell selection (A3/A5 gap), 200ms RA (weak beam), PDCP forwarding race condition.

enhancement_history.description: SN-side parameter mismatch root cause for NR-DC retainability failure — FR2 script gap analysis + mmWave diffo
changes: Added 10 T3 nodes: SN baseline params (CgSwitch, SSB beams, t310, HARQ, rsrpCritical, dcDlAggCgPrio, MCPC, CA meas) + FR1/FR2 script configs
Added 2 T4 nodes: CG Switch counters, MCPC PSCell Change counters
Added 1 T5 KPI: CG Switch SCG→MCG Low Quality Ratio
Added 22 causal edges connecting SN baseline params to NR-DC failure modes
Added 2 playbooks: SN Baseline Audit (9-step) + FR2 Script Enhancement (6-step)
Added 2 causal chains: SN_BASELINE_MISMATCH + FR2_SCRIPT_GAP
Added diffo reference data to LAH0050 site deployment
Added NRDC activation script metadata to LAH0050 site deployment
enhancement_history.key_finding: FR2 activation script (v3) activates 4 licenses but configures ZERO SN-side parameters. The venue mmWave baseline has 9 critical parameter mismatches vs reference node (LAD100BA) that are hostile to NR-DC SCG stability. Root cause of systematic NR-DC retainability failure at Dodger Stadium and likely all venues using this FR2 template.

nodes_T1.name: VSWR / Return Loss Alarm
nodes_T1.description: Voltage Standing Wave Ratio exceeds market threshold — impedance mismatch at antenna port
nodes_T1.amos_check: lh ru fui get vswr; hget . vswrSupervisionActive|vswrSupervisionSensitivity; get RfPort=* vswrMinorThreshold,vswrMajorThreshold
nodes_T1.field_detection: Sweep test with cable analyzer; Ericsson AMOS: lh ru fui get vswr; hget . vswrSupervisionActive|vswrSupervisionSensitivity

mmwave_deployed_validation.mmwave_note: mmWave active antennas (AIR5331/AIR5322) have integrated VSWR monitoring. VSWR thresholds tighter for mmWave due to higher frequency losses.

nodes_T1.name: RSSI Hot Threshold Breach
nodes_T1.description: RSSI above hot threshold — DAS over-driving, amplifier saturation risk, PIM generation
nodes_T1.amos_check: pmx (live per-cell RSSI Rx0/Rx1); invfrx (live UL interference level)

LAYH023A.note: DAS venue with AILG-controlled power levels

mmwave_deployed_validation.note: RSSI on mmWave (N258/N260) has different noise floor. Indoor mmWave heavily attenuated. Hot RSSI from inter-site interference at stadium seam zones between DAS 1/2/3.

nodes_T1.name: RSSI Cold Threshold Breach
nodes_T1.description: RSSI below cold threshold — insufficient UL gain, dead amplifier, antenna disconnect

mmwave_deployed_validation.note: Cold RSSI on mmWave expected beyond ~100m from active antenna due to high free-space path loss at 24/39GHz.

nodes_T1.name: RSSI Branch Imbalance
nodes_T1.description: Rx branch 0 vs branch 1 delta exceeds 3 dB — antenna path fault
nodes_T1.amos_check: pmx (live per-branch RSSI for |Rx0-Rx1| delta); get RfBranch=* ulAttenuation; lget AntennaBranch=* antennaSupervisionThreshold
nodes_T1.impact_summary: Directly degrades MIMO Rank 2 performance, reduces DL throughput by up to 30%

nodes_T1.name: Fiber Loss / Rx Power Alarm
nodes_T1.description: BBU-to-RRU fiber loss outside +3 to -3 dB or SFP Rx power below -5 dBm
nodes_T1.impact_summary: Causes bit errors on fronthaul (CPRI/eCPRI), increased BLER, scheduling gaps, throughput degradation

mmwave_deployed_validation.note: mmWave requires 24.3Gb/s SFP+ (2.4x sub-6). Fiber loss more critical due to higher data rates. BB6648 uses separate fiber paths from RP6651.

nodes_T1.name: PIM (Passive Intermodulation) Detection
nodes_T1.description: Intermodulation products from corroded connectors, loose hardware, or dissimilar metals in RF path
nodes_T1.detection: PIM analyzer sweep; manifests as 3-10 dB SINR degradation with no change in RSRP

nodes_T1.name: RTWP / UL Interference Alarm
nodes_T1.description: Received Total Wideband Power exceeds -95 dBm — UL interference from PIM, external source, or DAS amplifier oscillation
nodes_T1.amos_check: invfrx (live per-sector RTWP — one sector elevated = PIM/connector; all sectors = external interference/DAS oscillation); get RfBranch=* ulAttenuation

nodes_T1.name: Radio Unit Hardware Fault
nodes_T1.description: BB or RU operational state degraded — critical alarm on hardware
nodes_T1.amos_check: hget FieldReplaceableUnit=* operationalState|hwModel; st Radio; al (active alarms — distinguish RU Internal Hardware Failure from Fronthaul Connectivity Loss)

LAYH023A.fiber_type: BB/RP to CSR SM LC-LC 10G Opt cable (2m and 4m)

mmwave_deployed_validation.note: Active antennas (AIR5331/AIR5322) combine radio+antenna. Thermal alarms more common for mmWave due to PA heat dissipation.

nodes_T1.name: GPS / Sync Loss Alarm
nodes_T1.description: GPS receiver failure or SyncE/PTP loss causing TDD timing misalignment
nodes_T1.amos_check: lst ntp|ptp|sync|gps|clock; get RadioEquipmentClock=* radioClockState (RNT_TIME_LOCKED=healthy, HOLDOVER=recent loss, FREERUN=sync lost — TDD UL/DL collision imminent); al | grep -i sync

LAYH023A.gps_kit: Universal GPS Kit for NSD and PTP/ToPP
cables: IXRe to GNSS DIN 1,0/2,3RA to N-Type 2M
IXRe to GNSS SMA(m) to N-Type(m) 5m

nodes_T1.name: Backhaul / Fronthaul Transport Fault
nodes_T1.description: S1/NG interface failure, SCTP association failure, eCPRI sync loss, BH congestion

LAYH023A.dac_cables: DAC Cable SFP+_SFP+ 10G Baseband to IXRe (x3)

baseband_types.BB6648: Dedicated mmWave baseband. 3 per site. Handles SCS=120kHz.

mmwave_deployed_validation.note: BB6648 transport alarms specific to mmWave. CSR IXRe V2 (Gen2) transport with SCTP for AMF connectivity.

nodes_T1.name: DAS Amplifier Saturation / Oscillation
nodes_T1.description: DAS remote unit or BDA amplifier operating above 1 dB compression point — generates broadband noise, clips signal peaks, creates spectral regrowth
nodes_T1.detection: Input power exceeding DAS manufacturer spec; cascaded NF > 6 dB; RSSI Hot on all bands simultaneously
common_causes: Headend output too high after carrier addition
nodes_T1.source: RF_RAG Section 2.16.1 (AILG context); Ericsson_Guide Section 3.1.1 (RTWP sources)

nodes_T1.name: Antenna Tilt / Azimuth Mismatch
nodes_T1.description: Physical or electrical tilt not matching CIQ/design; azimuth offset > 5° from planned bearing
nodes_T1.impact: Creates coverage holes or overshooting causing pilot pollution and increased interference
nodes_T1.detection: Field audit comparing actual vs CIQ tilt/azimuth; walk test showing coverage gaps at expected locations
nodes_T1.source: Ericsson_Guide Section 3.1.1 (coverage domain); RF_RAG Section 2.1 (RSRP degradation causes)

nodes_T1.name: Missing Feature License / Activation
nodes_T1.description: Critical feature licensed but not activated (licenseState=ENABLED, featureState=DEACTIVATED), or license expired/missing

key_features.CXC4012324: EN-DC (NSA) — if featureState=DEACTIVATED, no EN-DC on this cell
key_features.NR_CA: NR Carrier Aggregation — if missing, single-carrier throughput ceiling
key_features.VoNR: Voice over NR — if missing, all calls fall back to LTE

nodes_T1.amos_check: lget Lm featureState|licenseState|description; lget Lm featureState|licenseState | grep -v ACTIVATED; al | grep -i licens
nodes_T1.source: PAG_Causation Section 6 (EN-DC feature check); Ericsson_Guide Section 1.1

nodes_T1.name: Cell Outage / AdminState Locked
nodes_T1.description: NRCellDU or NRCellCU administrativeState=LOCKED or operationalState=DISABLED — cell not providing service
nodes_T1.amos_check: lget NRCellCU administrativeState|operationalState (state table showing adminState + opState); lget NRCellDU administrativeState|operationalState; lget NRCellCU administrativeState|operationalState; ldeb NRCellDU=<cell_id> (unlock if LOCKED/ENABLED); al | grep -iE cell|lock|NRCell

nodes_T1.name: PCI Collision / Confusion
nodes_T1.description: Two cells with same PCI visible to UE (collision) or same mod-3/mod-30 causing DMRS/CSI-RS conflicts (confusion)
nodes_T1.detection: Walk test showing unexpected handover failures; ENM PCI audit report
nodes_T1.impact: UE cannot distinguish cells → measurement reporting errors → HO failures → drops
nodes_T1.source: Ericsson_Guide Section 3.3 (Mobility/HO failures); RF_RAG Section 2.9 (PCI pollution in scanner data)

LAYH023A.note: Consecutive PCI allocation (930-935), mod3 values: [0,1,2,0,1,2] — no mod3 collision risk within site

mmwave_deployed_validation.note: 76 NR cells across venue. mmWave PCIs intentionally separated from sub-6. PCI reuse across N258 channels OK since different ARFCNs.

nodes_T1.name: Multi-gNB Venue Architecture
nodes_T1.description: Large venue deployment with multiple gNBs sharing a TAC and coordinated PCI/VLAN plan. Angel Stadium uses 4 gNBs (IEY0208A-0211A) with 7 NR nodes and up to 95 N258 cells.

nodes_T1.name: VSWR Supervision Active
nodes_T1.description: Voltage Standing Wave Ratio monitoring on DAS RF ports. Feature CXC4012024 with sensitivity=100 detects antenna system degradation (cable damage, connector issues, water ingress) before it impacts coverage.

nodes_T1.name: External Alarm Integration
nodes_T1.description: 8-port external alarm monitoring for venue environmental conditions: climate faults, door access, commercial power loss, battery CB trip, surge suppressor failure, generator low fuel. All with 60s filter delay.

thresholds.mop_pass: > 25 dB for both Radio End and Antenna End

nodes_T2.name: MIMO Rank / RX Chain Status
nodes_T2.service_mode_field: NR RX0-RX3 RSRP, NR_ANT MAX/MIN RSRP

diagnostics.2_strong_2_at_neg140: One polarization path dead — Rank 2 only
diagnostics.delta_gt_15dB: UE antenna imbalance or DAS cross-pol issue
diagnostics.all_at_neg140: UE not receiving NR — check RU, license, activation

nodes_T2.name: Timing Advance (TA)

interpretation.macro_normal: TA proportional to distance (1 TA ≈ 78m for LTE)
interpretation.abnormal_high: TA > 10 in DAS = UE on wrong cell (outdoor macro bleeding in)
interpretation.abnormal_zero_outdoor: TA 0 at cell edge = timing issue

nodes_T2.diagnostic_use: TA distribution helps identify UEs served by wrong cell in venue

nodes_T2.name: Carrier Aggregation Activation Status

expected.LTE_2CC: SCell activated when primary RSRP > -100 dBm and SCell RSRP > -100 dBm
expected.NR_2C: Second carrier active in NR-DC or SA-CA when configured

ca_not_activating_causes: SCell RSRP too weak (< -100 dBm for LTE, vendor-specific for NR)
UE capability doesn't support band combination
nodes_T2.source: RF_RAG Section 2.10 (CA status); RF_RAG Section 9.2.5 (CA KPI mapping)

nodes_T2.name: NR Band / EARFCN Identification
nodes_T2.diagnostic_use: Confirm UE is on expected carrier for location; identify if UE stuck on wrong band
nodes_T2.source: RF_RAG Section 2.9; PAG_Causation Section 5 (DCSC band steering)

nodes_T2.name: PCI Collision / Confusion
nodes_T2.description: Physical Cell Identity collision occurs when two cells in the same measurement area share a PCI, causing UE measurement ambiguity. In multi-gNB venues, sequential PCI allocation (e.g., 882-902 across 7 nodes) prevents collision but must be validated against neighboring macro sites.

nodes_T2.name: SSB 120kHz SCS Configuration
nodes_T2.description: N258 mmWave uses 120kHz SSB subcarrier spacing (vs 30kHz for sub-6). This determines beam sweep timing and measurement periodicity. Misconfigured SSB SCS causes UE synchronization failure.

values.3: VoNR enabled — voice on NR with 5QI-1 bearers
values.5: EPS Fallback — redirect to LTE for voice, adds 200-500ms latency

LAYH023A.note: CSFB enabled at IX; epsFallback tuning (op3/5) applied in PAG post-integration

mmwave_deployed_defaults.note: EPS Fallback critical for mmWave VoNR. Cross-node handover NLAY→MLAY for VoLTE when mmWave blocked.

nodes_T3.name: PCell A5 Mobility Thresholds
kpi_impact: SA HO Intra-Freq SR (>90% venue, >95% macro)
SA NGBR Drop Rate (<2% venue, <0.5% macro)

LAYH023A.note: A5 PCell thresholds for L2100↔L1900 inter-freq configured in 02_LTE_LTE_Relation_Parameters_MLAY2301A.mos

mmwave_deployed_defaults.note: mmWave RSRP drops ~20dB over 100m vs ~6dB for sub-6. Tighter A5 thresholds needed.

nodes_T3.name: PSCell A5 Thresholds (NR-to-NR)
nodes_T3.correction: PSCell uses A5 events, NOT B2. B2 with threshold2EUtra is PCell-only for inter-RAT

K_N71.insight: threshold1=-80 effectively disables normal A5 PSCell changes on N71; changes driven by rsrpCritical (-116, TTT=160ms) instead

LAYH023A.note: PSCell A5 for NR-to-NR carrier switching configured in 12 relation scripts

mmwave_deployed_defaults.note: PSCell A5 for EN-DC/NR-DC. mmWave PSCell removal threshold lower due to volatility.

nodes_T3.name: rsrpCritical Emergency Mobility
nodes_T3.critical_note: Last line of defense before RLF in SA mode. If rsrpCriticalEnabled=false, UEs will RLF instead of attempting last-resort PCell change

nodes_T3.name: endcAllowedPlmnList / MOCN Control
kpi_impact: NSA SN Addition FR (<1% venue, <0.5% macro)

LAYH023A.note: B1 thresholds for EN-DC addition configured in MLAY2301A_LTE_N19_Relation.mos, MLAY2301A_LTE_N2500_1C/2C_Relation.mos

mmwave_deployed_defaults.note: N258 SCS=120kHz measurement with smtcPeriodicity=20ms. Dummy_freq_mmw24_xC pattern for 5 channels.

126510_N41.insight: N41 is NEVER pCellAllowed — serves as SCell only

nodes_T3.name: MLC_Problematic (ML Disabled for Problem UEs)
nodes_T3.correction: MLC_Problematic DISABLES ML entirely (mlcEnabled=false), does NOT use conservative ML

N25_1C.insight: ONLY N25 1C gets RWR — best capacity/coverage balance for FDD

nodes_T3.name: Closed Loop Power Control (CLPC / TPC)
nodes_T3.description: Uplink and downlink power control parameters controlling UE TX power and gNB scheduling power

key_params.pZeroNominalPusch: Target UL received power at gNB (typically -96 to -106 dBm)
key_params.msg3PowerOffsetGroupB: RACH Msg3 power offset — affects UL initial access
key_params.p0NominalPUSCH: NR equivalent — open-loop UL power target
key_params.tpcAccumulation: True=accumulated TPC, False=absolute TPC (DAS may need absolute)

nodes_T3.source: PAG_Causation Section 12.1 (CLPC listed as primary_param for RACH); Ericsson_Guide Section 3.1.2

nodes_T3.name: QoS / 5QI Configuration
nodes_T3.description: 5G QoS Identifier mapping — determines scheduling priority, packet delay budget, and error rate targets
nodes_T3.source: PAG_Causation Section 12.1 (5QI config as primary_param); Ericsson_Guide Section 2.1 (QoS mapping)

LAYH023A.note: 5QI-to-QoS mapping tables for both MMBB and standalone gNB

nodes_T3.name: DSS (Dynamic Spectrum Sharing) Configuration
nodes_T3.description: Dynamic sharing of LTE/NR on same carrier — reduces available PRBs for both technologies
nodes_T3.impact_chain: DSS ON → fewer NR PRBs available → reduced NR DL scheduling % → lower throughput
nodes_T3.key_consideration: DSS cells show 15-30% throughput reduction vs dedicated NR carrier of same BW
nodes_T3.detection: NR DL Scheduling % < 50% on DSS carrier while LTE has moderate load
nodes_T3.source: Ericsson_Guide Section 4.4 (DSS troubleshooting); RF_RAG Section 2.6 (scheduling interpretation)

nodes_T3.name: coverageIndicator (Venue vs Macro Cell Type)

values.1: Venue/indoor — DAS or small cell, limited coverage

nodes_T3.impact: Determines which KPI thresholds apply (ACS Venue vs MS2 Macro — different targets)
nodes_T3.misconfig_risk: If coverageIndicator=0 on a DAS cell, KPI dashboards will apply wrong (macro) thresholds — issues go undetected
nodes_T3.source: PAG_Causation Section 12.1 (listed as primary_param for SA RRC Setup SR)

LAYH023A.note: All cells configured as omnidirectional DAS (0° tilt, 360° opening)

mmwave_deployed_defaults.note: N258 indoor range ~100m. Stadium bowl geometry. 3 DAS sites partition venue.

nodes_T3.name: ANR / SON Configuration
nodes_T3.description: Automatic Neighbor Relations and SON features — auto-discover and manage neighbor cell relations

key_params.anrEnabled: If false, no automatic NR frequency relations created
key_params.anrUeCoverageCheckEnabled: Verify UE coverage before adding relation
key_params.cellRelationType: AUTO vs MANUAL — AUTO can create unwanted outdoor→indoor relations

nodes_T3.impact: Missing NR freq relations → HO preparation failure → HO SR degrades → drops increase

LAYH023A.note: ANR measurement disabled at IX; SON/ANR enablement is post-integration PAG step

mmwave_deployed_defaults.note: xnIpAddrViaNgActive disabled during IX relation creation, re-enabled after.

nodes_T3.name: PRACH Configuration (Random Access)
nodes_T3.description: PRACH format, root sequence index, preamble resources — determines RACH capacity and coverage

key_params.prachConfigIndex: Determines PRACH occasion periodicity (more occasions = more RACH capacity)
key_params.rootSequenceIndex: Must be unique per cell to avoid PRACH collision
key_params.preambleTransMax: Max preamble retransmissions (default 10) — increase for UL-challenged DAS
key_params.powerRampingStep: dB increase per RACH retry (2/4/6 dB) — higher for deep indoor

LAYH023A.note: Unique RACH root sequence per sector to avoid PRACH collision in tunnel

mmwave_deployed_defaults.note: SCS=120kHz PRACH. 3 overlapping DAS sites need careful RACH planning.

nodes_T3.name: nrDcAllowed / NR-DC Activation Control
nodes_T3.description: Controls whether NR Dual Connectivity is permitted on a cell. When nrDcAllowed=false on GNBCUCPFunction→NRCellCU, the cell cannot participate in NR-DC as either MN or SN. This is the single-parameter kill switch for NR-DC — equivalent to endcAllowedPlmnList for EN-DC.
nodes_T3.fail_condition: nrDcAllowed=false on any cell that should support NR-DC
nodes_T3.impact: 100% NR-DC setup failure on affected cell — no SCG addition possible
nodes_T3.source: Ericsson CPI NRCellCU, LAH0050 Dodger Stadium IX analysis
nodes_T3.dodger_stadium_context: Must be true on all MLAY N19/N25 cells (MN role) AND all NLAY N258 cells (SN role). Check across all 3 sites (LAY5501A/5502A/5503A).

nodes_T3.name: SCG Failure Handling Configuration
nodes_T3.description: SCG (Secondary Cell Group) failure handling parameters determine UE behavior when the NR-DC secondary leg fails. Includes t310 (RLF detection timer on SCG), t311 (RLF recovery timer), n310/n311 counters, and scgFailureInformationHandling on GNBCUCPFunction. Critical for mmWave PSCell where beam failures are frequent.

t310_scg_ms.description: SCG Radio Link Failure detection timer

n310.description: Consecutive out-of-sync indications to start t310

n311.description: Consecutive in-sync indications to stop t310

scgFailureInformationHandling.description: Whether MN requests SCG release or re-addition after SCG failure info

nodes_T3.impact: Aggressive timers (short t310) cause frequent SCG releases on mmWave. Conservative timers delay failure detection.
nodes_T3.source: 3GPP TS 38.331 RRC, Ericsson CPI SCG Failure Handling
nodes_T3.dodger_stadium_context: mmWave at 24/39GHz has sub-second blockage events from human body. t310=1000ms may be too aggressive — recommend 2000ms for stadium with 56K capacity.

nodes_T3.name: Conditional PSCell Change (CPC) Configuration
nodes_T3.description: Conditional PSCell Change allows UE to pre-evaluate candidate PSCells and switch before the current PSCell degrades below threshold. Critical for mmWave where signal drops are instantaneous. When CPC is disabled, every mmWave beam loss forces full SCG release + re-addition cycle (200-500ms gap).

cpcThresholdRsrp.description: RSRP threshold to trigger CPC evaluation

cpcOffset.description: Candidate PSCell must be this much better than threshold

nodes_T3.impact: Without CPC, mmWave NR-DC has 30-50% higher SCG failure rate due to abrupt beam loss

nodes_T3.name: NR-DC Band Combination Configuration
nodes_T3.description: NR-DC band combination table controls which NR band pairs can form dual connectivity. At Dodger Stadium: N19+N258, N25+N258, N41+N258, N19+N25 are the valid combinations across MLAY/NLAY/NLAY2. Missing band combinations cause silent NR-DC failure — no alarm, no error, the setup request simply never initiates.
nodes_T3.impact: Missing band combination = 0% NR-DC on that band pair with zero alarms
nodes_T3.source: Ericsson CPI NrDcBandCombination, LAH0050 CIQ analysis

nodes_T3.name: TermPointToGNB / Xn Interface Configuration
nodes_T3.description: Xn interface between gNBs is required for NR-DC signaling (SCG addition/modification/release). Configured via ExternalGNBCUCPFunction + TermPointToGNB on each node. Must be bidirectional — if MLAY has ExternalGNBCUCPFunction for NLAY but NLAY doesn't have reciprocal, NR-DC forms in one direction only.
nodes_T3.amos_check: get ExternalGNBCUCPFunction=* gNBId|administrativeState; get TermPointToGNB=* ipAddress|domainRef
nodes_T3.impact: Missing/misconfigured Xn = NR-DC impossible between affected node pair
nodes_T3.source: LAH0050 IX Scripts, Ericsson CPI ExternalGNBCUCPFunction

nodes_T3.name: NRCellRelation DC-Related Parameters
nodes_T3.description: NR cell relation parameters that control NR-DC candidate cell selection. Retrieved via hget nrcellrel. Includes: caStatus (CA/DC status on relation), isHoAllowed (handover allowed), cellCand (cell candidate for mobility/DC), coverageInd (coverage indicator), cellIndividualOffsetNR (CIO for NR-NR). Misconfigured relations cause specific NR-DC paths to fail while others succeed.

caStatus.description: CA/DC status on this relation — controls whether DC is attempted

isHoAllowed.description: Whether HO/DC is allowed on this relation

cellCand.description: Cell candidate status for mobility/DC evaluation

coverageIndicator.description: Coverage indicator on neighbor relation — affects DC selection priority

cellIndividualOffsetNR.description: CIO offset applied to NR-NR measurements — shifts DC threshold per-relation

nodes_T3.impact: Wrong caStatus or isHoAllowed=false on a relation silently blocks NR-DC on that cell pair
nodes_T3.source: Ericsson AMOS hget nrcellrel, LAH0050 venue relation analysis

nodes_T3.name: xnIpAddrViaNgActive Toggle Pattern
nodes_T3.description: During IX relation creation, xnIpAddrViaNgActive must be DISABLED (false) to allow manual Xn configuration, then RE-ENABLED (true) after relations are committed. If left disabled post-IX, the node cannot dynamically discover Xn peers — blocking NR-DC for any node pair not explicitly configured. This is a common post-IX residual error.
nodes_T3.impact: Dynamic Xn peer discovery disabled — only manually configured Xn peers can form NR-DC. At multi-site venue, this may block cross-site NR-DC entirely.
nodes_T3.dodger_stadium_context: All 9 gNBs (3 MLAY + 3 NLAY + 3 NLAY2) must have xnIpAddrViaNgActive=true post-IX

nodes_T3.name: tSCGconfigTimer (Ericsson SN Change Guard Timer)
nodes_T3.description: Ericsson proprietary MN-side guard timer that fires if the new SCG does not achieve stable operation within the configured window after SN Change (PSCell change). If set too aggressively, the MN releases the new SCG before the mmWave beam stabilizes — causing immediate teardown post-SN Change. This was identified as root cause #4 in the Dodger Stadium NR-DC protocol trace analysis (15ms collapse window).
nodes_T3.fail_condition: Timer too short for mmWave RA + beam stabilization. If mmWave RA takes 200ms (multiple preamble retries), timer must be > 200ms + beam stabilization margin.
nodes_T3.impact: Immediate SCG release after successful SN Change — NR-DC setup succeeds but collapses in <15ms
nodes_T3.source: Dodger Stadium protocol trace analysis (Sasanka Valluri, 3/17/2026), Root Cause #4

protocol_evidence.interpretation: 15ms is too short for even a timer — this is either an extremely aggressive tSCGconfigTimer or an immediate SCG failure detection

nodes_T3.recommendation: Increase tSCGconfigTimer to accommodate mmWave RA duration (200ms+) plus beam stabilization (~100ms). Minimum recommended: 500ms for mmWave venues.

nodes_T3.name: SN Change A3/A5 Trigger vs Sustain Threshold Gap
nodes_T3.description: The threshold gap between 'good enough to trigger SN Change' (A3/A5 measurement event) and 'good enough to sustain post-change'. At Dodger Stadium, the second MeasReport at :316 (1ms after rrcReconfiguration) showed the new SN cell already degrading — indicating the A3/A5 threshold that triggered the SN Change was too close to the release threshold. This creates a 'marginal PSCell selection' problem where mmWave cells briefly appear viable during beam sweep but cannot sustain a link.

parameters.a3Offset_for_SN_change: Offset for A3 event triggering SN Change — must be large enough that new SN is significantly better
parameters.hysteresisA3: Hysteresis to prevent ping-pong SN Changes
parameters.timeToTriggerA3: Time the A3 condition must hold before triggering — too short catches transient beams

nodes_T3.amos_check: get NRCellCU=* a3Offset|hysteresisA3|timeToTriggerA3; hget nrcellrel cellIndividualOffsetNR
nodes_T3.fail_condition: A3 offset too small (< 3dB) or timeToTrigger too short (< 320ms) for mmWave
nodes_T3.impact: SN Change triggers on transient mmWave beams that cannot sustain link → immediate SCG failure post-change
nodes_T3.source: Dodger Stadium protocol trace, Root Cause #2

protocol_evidence.interpretation: UE reporting new SN degrading 1ms after receiving reconfig → cell was already failing at selection time

nodes_T3.name: beamSweepTimerDuration / SSB Periodicity on mmWave SN
nodes_T3.description: Beam sweep timer and SSB (Synchronization Signal Block) periodicity on the mmWave SN cell. Controls how quickly the UE can find and lock onto a beam during RA on the new PSCell after SN Change. If SSB periodicity is too long or beam sweep too slow, the 200ms RA duration (multiple preamble retries) indicates the UE struggled to find a viable beam — suggesting weak coverage at the SN Change location.
nodes_T3.amos_check: get NRSectorCarrier=* ssbPeriodicity|beamSweepTimer*; get NRCellDU=* ssbPeriodicity
nodes_T3.fail_condition: 200ms RA duration with multiple preamble retries = weak beam at SN Change target
nodes_T3.impact: Prolonged RA on new SN delays SCG establishment, may exceed tSCGconfigTimer → immediate release
nodes_T3.source: Dodger Stadium protocol trace, Root Cause #1 + Diagnostic Action #4

nodes_T3.name: PDCP Forwarding / SNStatusTransfer During SN Change
nodes_T3.description: During SN Change, the MN forwards PDCP SN (Sequence Number) status from old SN to new SN via XnAP/SNStatusTransfer. If forwarding doesn't complete before the new SN starts scheduling, PDCP state inconsistency causes RLC resets and bearer failure. In the Dodger Stadium trace, SNStatusTransfer appears in BOTH the setup and teardown sequences — suggesting the transfer may not have completed cleanly.
nodes_T3.amos_check: No direct AMOS parameter — requires XnAP trace decode to verify PDCP forwarding completion
nodes_T3.fail_condition: SNStatusTransfer during teardown = PDCP state inconsistency
nodes_T3.impact: SN Change completes at RRC layer but PDCP state mismatch causes immediate data plane failure → SCG release
nodes_T3.source: Dodger Stadium protocol trace, Root Cause #3

protocol_evidence.interpretation: SNStatusTransfer appearing in both directions suggests forwarding race condition

nodes_T3.diagnostic: Decode XnAP messages — check if SNStatusTransfer in teardown carries different SN counts than setup, indicating incomplete forwarding

nodes_T3.name: DL Cell Group Switch Mode

dlCgSwitchMode.impact: Enables constant MCG↔SCG data path switching; destabilizes NR-DC SCG leg

dlScgLowQualThresh.impact: Threshold=50 triggers SCG→MCG switch at virtually any quality; reference -400 effectively never triggers

nodes_T3.nrdc_impact: CRITICAL — CG switching enabled with permissive threshold causes MN to continuously pull data off SCG during NR-DC, undermining the dual connectivity benefit and creating signaling storms

nodes_T3.name: SSB Beam Configuration (CommonBeamforming)

ssbBeams.impact: 24 narrow beams vs default; more frequent beam transitions

usedSsbBeams.impact: Double the active SSB beams; narrower per-beam coverage requires more aggressive beam management

nodes_T3.nrdc_impact: HIGH — 24 narrow SSB beams on mmWave create volatile beam environment. Post-SN Change, UE on new PSCell has narrower beam to track. Beam failure → SCG collapse if t310 is too short

nodes_T3.name: t310 Radio Link Failure Timer

t310.impact: 3x more aggressive RLF timer; mmWave beam recovery often needs >2s

nodes_T3.nrdc_impact: CRITICAL — t310=2000ms on SN means beam failure during/after SN Change has only 2s to recover before RLF. With 24 narrow SSB beams and 200ms RA, recovery window is insufficient. Direct cause of rapid SCG release

nodes_T3.name: DL HARQ Mode

dlHarqMode.impact: Normal HARQ has fewer retransmission opportunities; mmWave burst errors need robust mode

nodes_T3.nrdc_impact: MEDIUM — HARQ_NORMAL on mmWave SN gives up faster on failed transmissions. During SN Change transition when beam is marginal, normal HARQ → higher BLER → faster quality degradation → CG switch or RLF

nodes_T3.name: RSRP Critical PSCell Monitoring

rsrpCritical_threshold.impact: Reference monitors PSCell RSRP and triggers change at -112; venue has no monitoring

nodes_T3.nrdc_impact: CRITICAL — With rsrpCriticalEnabled=false, MN has NO proactive mechanism to detect degrading PSCell and initiate graceful SN Change. Instead of preemptive handoff, PSCell rides into ground → abrupt SCG failure. This is the root cause of the 14ms collapse pattern

nodes_T3.name: DC DL Aggregation CG Priority

dcDlAggCgPrio.impact: Equal priority doesn't leverage mmWave SCG capacity; SCG_PRIO directs DL data to high-bandwidth leg

nodes_T3.nrdc_impact: MEDIUM — EQUAL_PRIO means MN splits DL across MCG and SCG equally instead of leveraging mmWave capacity. Reduces NR-DC throughput benefit and creates unnecessary MCG load

nodes_T3.name: MCPC PCell/PSCell Feature Enable

mcpcPSCellEnabled.impact: Disables MCPC PSCell coordination for NR-DC

nodes_T3.nrdc_impact: HIGH — MCPC (Multi-Cell/Multi-Panel Connectivity) disabled means no multi-panel beam coordination during NR-DC. The SN cannot coordinate beams across panels for PSCell, reducing beam diversity and increasing beam failure probability

nodes_T3.name: CA SCell Measurement Thresholds

rsrpBetterSCell_offset.impact: 5x less offset → triggers on minimal improvement

rsrpSCellCoverage_threshold.impact: Essentially no coverage floor; allows SCell on unusable signal

nodes_T3.nrdc_impact: HIGH — Extremely aggressive CA meas config creates constant SCell add/remove churn on the SN's mmWave carriers. During NR-DC, this CA instability on the SCG compounds with beam volatility to create a perpetually unstable SCG leg

nodes_T3.name: NR-DC FR1 MN Activation Parameters

nrdcSnTermAllowed.meaning: All QoS flows allowed as SN termination

nodes_T3.nrdc_impact: BASELINE — This is the MN-side NR-DC activation. rsrpPSCellCandidate at -113 with TTT=320ms is the entry threshold. McpcNrdcPSCellProfile rsrpCritical at -119 with 160ms TTT controls when MN considers PSCell critical. These values are reasonable. The gap is on the SN side.

nodes_T3.name: NR-DC FR2 SN Activation (License-Only Gap)
missing_sn_parameters: dlCgSwitchMode not set → remains ENABLED from baseline
dlScgLowQualThresh not set → remains 50 from baseline
t310 not set → remains 2000ms from baseline
dlHarqMode not set → remains HARQ_NORMAL from baseline
rsrpCriticalEnabled not set → remains false from baseline
CA meas thresholds not tuned → remain aggressive
nodes_T3.nrdc_impact: ROOT CAUSE — FR2 script activates 4 licenses but configures ZERO SN-side parameters. The SN's baseline template (venue-optimized mmWave DAS config) is designed for standalone throughput, not NR-DC stability. Every parameter that matters for SCG retainability is left at the aggressive venue default.

nodes_T3.name: Venue PCI Allocation Plan
nodes_T3.description: Coordinated PCI assignment across all gNBs in a multi-node venue. IEYH002A uses sequential blocks: IEY0208A(882-889), IEY0209A(890-892), IEY0210A(896-898), IEY0211A(902). Non-contiguous gaps indicate reserved PCIs for future expansion.

nodes_T3.name: Shared Tracking Area Code
nodes_T3.description: All venue gNBs share a single TAC (3238400 at Angel Stadium) enabling seamless mobility within the venue without tracking area updates. Critical for venue UE density management.

nodes_T3.name: CORENET VLAN Isolation
nodes_T3.description: User plane traffic isolation via separate CORENET VLANs per node group. IEYH002A: IEY0209A/0210A share VLAN 2267, IEY0211A uses VLAN 2313. Misconfigured VLANs cause user plane routing failures.

nodes_T3.name: Multi-AMF Redundancy
nodes_T3.description: Each gNB connects to 16 AMFs across 6+ regional pools (LA, SD, IE, SV, EL, IR). All configured as default=true with adminState=LOCKED (pre-integration). Provides geographic AMF failover for venue resilience.

nodes_T3.name: 5-Cell-Per-Sector mmWave Config
nodes_T3.description: N258 venue sectors use 5 cells per sector spanning the 2480-2520MHz range (100MHz per cell via NRARFCNs 2025833-2032499). First 2 cells at 200W, remaining 3 at 133.3W. This maximizes N258 spectrum utilization for venue capacity.

nodes_T3.name: RACH Root Sequence Separation
nodes_T3.description: Each sector uses a unique RACH root sequence to prevent preamble collision. In multi-gNB venues, root sequences must be separated by at least the cyclic shift range to avoid cross-sector false detections.

nodes_T3.name: Differentiated Node Configuration
nodes_T3.description: IEY0211A is configured differently from IEY0209A/0210A: only 5 cells (vs 15), separate CORENET VLAN (2313 vs 2267), NR_CIQ type (vs MMBB_CIQ), and explicit LTE B7 fallback (EARFCN 396250). This indicates a different role — likely a macro-facing or coverage-extension node rather than capacity DAS.

nodes_T4.name: RACH Msg1 Out-of-Coverage Failures
nodes_T4.meaning: UE PRACH preamble too weak — path loss exceeds UL link budget

nodes_T4.name: NR-DC Setup Counters (MN & SN perspective)
nodes_T4.description: NR-DC setup attempt/success counters from both Master Node and Secondary Node perspectives. These are the primary counters for diagnosing NR-DC call failure rate.

counters.pmNrdcSetupUeMnAtt: NR-DC setup attempts where this cell is Master Node (MN initiates DC)
counters.pmNrdcSetupUeMnSucc: NR-DC setup successes from MN perspective
counters.pmNrdcSetupUeSnAtt: NR-DC setup attempts where this cell is Secondary Node (SN receives DC request)
counters.pmNrdcSetupUeSnSucc: NR-DC setup successes from SN perspective

nodes_T4.source: Ericsson PM Counter Reference, AMOS pget command

nodes_T4.name: NR-DC Abnormal Release Counters
nodes_T4.description: NR-DC abnormal release counters — tracks SCG/MCG leg failures after successful NR-DC setup. Distinguishes SN-side vs MN-side failures and active vs inactive UE state at release.

counters.pmNrdcRelUeSnAbnormalMnInit: Abnormal NR-DC release on SN side, initiated by MN (MN detected SN failure)
counters.pmNrdcRelUeSnAbnormalMnInitAct: Same as above but UE was actively transmitting data at release time
counters.pmNrdcRelUeMnAbnormalMnInit: Abnormal NR-DC release on MN side, initiated by MN (MN-side failure during DC)
counters.pmNrdcRelUeMnAbnormalMnInitAct: Same as above but UE was actively transmitting

interpretation.high_SN_abnormal: mmWave PSCell failing — beam loss, RACH failure, RLC exhaustion on SCG leg
interpretation.high_MN_abnormal: Sub-6 PCell failing during DC — rare, indicates PCell coverage issue
interpretation.high_Act_ratio: Failures during active data = more user-impacting than idle DC failures

nodes_T4.normal_range: Abnormal release ratio < 15% of total NR-DC sessions (venue with mmWave)
nodes_T4.source: Ericsson PM Counter Reference, AMOS pmxeht command

nodes_T4.name: SCG Failure Detection Counters
nodes_T4.description: SCG (Secondary Cell Group) failure counters by cause. UE reports SCG failure information to MN which decides whether to release SCG or attempt recovery. Cause breakdown is essential for tuning t310/n310 and CPC parameters.

counters.pmScgFailureBeamFailure: SCG failure due to beam failure recovery exhaustion
counters.pmScgFailureRlcMaxRetx: SCG failure due to RLC max retransmission on SCG bearer
counters.pmScgFailureT310Expiry: SCG failure due to t310 timer expiry (radio link failure on SCG)
counters.pmScgFailureRachFailure: SCG failure due to RACH failure on PSCell

interpretation.beam_dominant: Beam failure dominant → check mmWave antenna tilt, coverage holes, CPC enablement
interpretation.rlc_dominant: RLC retx dominant → check BLER, SINR on SCG, interference from adjacent cells
interpretation.t310_dominant: t310 dominant → timer may be too aggressive for mmWave, consider increasing
interpretation.rach_dominant: RACH failure dominant → check PRACH config on PSCell, preamble collision

nodes_T4.name: Xn Interface Setup Counters
nodes_T4.description: Xn interface setup counters between gNBs. Xn is required for NR-DC signaling. If Xn setup fails between MLAY and NLAY, NR-DC cannot form regardless of RF conditions.

counters.pmXnSetupReq: Xn setup requests sent to peer gNB
counters.pmXnSetupResp: Xn setup responses received from peer

nodes_T4.alarm_level: Any Xn setup failure blocks NR-DC on that node pair

nodes_T4.name: Conditional PSCell Change Counters
nodes_T4.description: Counters for Conditional PSCell Change (CPC) feature. Tracks how often UEs successfully switch PSCell before beam loss vs. how often CPC fails and triggers SCG release.

counters.pmCpcSuccess: CPC completed successfully — PSCell switched without SCG release
counters.pmCpcFailRachFailure: CPC failed due to RACH failure on target PSCell
counters.pmCpcFailTimeout: CPC failed due to timeout — target PSCell degraded before switch

nodes_T4.source: Ericsson PM Counter Reference, 3GPP Rel-16 CPC

nodes_T4.name: SCG Change / SN Change Failure Counters
nodes_T4.description: Counters specifically for SN Change (PSCell change) failures — distinct from SN Addition. SN Change involves tearing down old SCG and establishing new SCG simultaneously, with PDCP forwarding. Failure here means NR-DC was working but PSCell mobility failed.

counters.pmNrScgChangeFail: SN Change failures — new SN could not sustain link
counters.pmNrScgFailureCause: SCG failure cause on the NEW SN (not old SN) — critical: failure attribution changes in SN Change context

nodes_T4.diagnostic_note: CRITICAL: Check pmNrScgFailureCause on the NEW SN, not old SN. In SN Change context, failure attribution shifts to the target cell.
nodes_T4.normal_range: SN Change SR > 90%. At mmWave venues with stadium-level blockage, > 80% acceptable.

nodes_T4.name: CG Switch Event Counters
nodes_T4.interpretation: High pmDlCgSwitchScgToMcgLowQual relative to pmDlCgSwitchMcgToScg indicates SCG quality instability. In NR-DC context, frequent SCG→MCG switches mean the mmWave PSCell is perceived as continuously degraded.

nodes_T4.name: MCPC PSCell Change Counters
nodes_T4.interpretation: With mcpcPSCellEnabled=false, these counters will be 0. After enabling MCPC, track PSCell change success rate. Low SR indicates beam/coverage issues on target PSCell.

nodes_T4.name: VSWR Alarm Count
nodes_T4.description: Count of VSWR threshold exceedances per RF port. Sustained VSWR alarms indicate DAS component degradation. Correlated with coverage holes and throughput drops in affected sectors.

nodes_T4.name: Tracking Area Update Attempts
nodes_T4.description: TAU attempts within the venue. A shared TAC should minimize these — elevated TAU counts indicate UEs crossing TAC boundaries or misconfigured TAC alignment across gNBs.

nodes_T4.name: RACH Preamble Collision Rate
nodes_T4.description: Rate of RACH preamble collisions within a cell. In dense venue deployments, insufficient root sequence separation or excessive UE density drives collision rate up, increasing access latency.

nodes_T4.name: XnAP SN Status Transfer
nodes_T4.description: XnAP SNStatusTransfer message during SN Change procedure. Carries PDCP SN status from source SN to target SN for lossless handover. Failure or delay at this step causes data loss during SN Change.

nodes_T4.name: RRC Reconfiguration Complete
nodes_T4.description: NR-RRC rrcReconfigurationComplete sent by UE after applying new SCG configuration during SN Change or NR-DC setup. Confirms UE has applied the new config. 14ms collapse after this message indicates immediate post-reconfig beam/quality failure.

NR_ACCESS.name: Venue RACH Success Rate
NR_ACCESS.description: Aggregate RACH success rate across all venue cells. Target >99% for stadium events. Degradation indicates root sequence collision, preamble exhaustion, or SSB sync issues.

NR_RETAIN.name: SA NGBR Data DRB Drop Rate

NR_AVAILABILITY.name: Venue Cell Availability
NR_AVAILABILITY.description: Percentage of cells operational across all venue gNBs. A 95-cell venue losing 15 cells (one gNB) drops to 84% — below the 95% target. Multi-gNB architecture provides resilience but requires coordinated monitoring.

NR_ENERGY_EFFICIENCY.name: DL Energy Efficiency (bits/J)
NR_ENERGY_EFFICIENCY.description: Bits delivered per Joule — affected by BWP config, DynPowerOpt, and cell load

NR_ENERGY_EFFICIENCY.name: DL Symbol Utilization (%)
NR_ENERGY_EFFICIENCY.description: Percentage of available DL symbols actually used for data scheduling

CROSS_DOMAIN.name: Composite UE Experience (Internal)
CROSS_DOMAIN.description: Weighted composite: 30% throughput + 25% accessibility + 25% retainability + 20% mobility

CROSS_DOMAIN.name: SA Call Setup Time (ms)
CROSS_DOMAIN.target: < 2000 ms for VoNR, < 500 ms for data

NR_DC.name: NR-DC Setup Success Rate
NR_DC.source: Ericsson PM Counters, Gap Analysis GAP-10

NR_DC.name: SCG Failure Rate (NR-DC Retainability)
NR_DC.source: Ericsson PM Counters, Gap Analysis GAP-11

NR_DC.name: SN Change (PSCell Mobility) Success Rate
NR_DC.source: Dodger Stadium NR-DC protocol trace analysis
NR_DC.note: Distinct from K_NRDC_SETUP_SR (which covers first-time SN Addition). Low SN Change SR with high NR-DC Setup SR = PSCell mobility problem, not accessibility problem.

NR_DC.name: CG Switch SCG→MCG Low Quality Ratio
NR_DC.interpretation: High ratio indicates the SCG (mmWave) leg is perceived as persistently low quality, triggering constant CG switches back to MCG. In NR-DC, this metric directly correlates with SCG retainability failures.

t310=2000ms on SN means beam failure has only 2s before RLF declaration → SCG release. With 200ms RA + beam sweep, recovery often exceeds 2s on mmWave
Short t310 on target SN causes RLF during SN Change before new SCG stabilizes
Aggressive t310 directly degrades SN Change success — new PSCell triggers RLF before beam lock
HARQ_NORMAL has fewer retransmission attempts; mmWave burst errors that HARQ_ROBUST would recover become NACKs
Higher BLER from normal HARQ degrades perceived SCG quality → contributes to CG switch and eventually SCG failure
rsrpCriticalEnabled=false means MN cannot detect degrading PSCell to trigger preemptive SN Change. PSCell degrades until abrupt failure — the 14ms collapse pattern
With rsrpCritical disabled, proactive PSCell change never triggers → counter stays at 0
No proactive PSCell management → all PSCell failures are abnormal releases rather than graceful changes
EQUAL_PRIO doesn't leverage mmWave SCG capacity for DL; throughput benefit of NR-DC is diluted
mcpcPSCellEnabled=false blocks MCPC PSCell coordination entirely; counter will be 0
Without MCPC multi-panel coordination, beam diversity is reduced → more beam failures on mmWave SN
Aggressive CA thresholds (hysteresis=10, TTT=160ms) cause constant SCell add/remove on mmWave SCG carriers
CA SCell churn on SCG creates signaling load and instability that compounds with NR-DC mobility events
FR2 script does not configure dlCgSwitchMode → venue baseline ENABLED persists
FR2 script does not configure t310 → venue baseline 2000ms persists
FR2 script does not configure rsrpCriticalEnabled → venue baseline false persists

mitigation_playbooks.title: VSWR / Return Loss Alarm Playbook
field_actions: Step 1: Identify affected sector/antenna port from alarm data (AMOS: get AntennaNearUnit=* returnLoss)
Step 2: PIM sweep on affected path using PIM analyzer
Step 3: Re-torque ALL N-type and 7-16 DIN connectors to 15-20 ft-lbs manufacturer spec
Step 4: Replace all connectors showing corrosion or dissimilar metal contact
Step 5: Replace any jumper cables older than 5 years or showing > 1 dB excess loss
Step 6: Use PIM-rated components (< -153 dBc) throughout RF path
Step 7: Post-fix: VSWR retest to confirm < threshold (2.1 for RRU 4402/2203, 1.4 for RRU 8863)
parameter_actions: None — VSWR is a physical issue, not a configuration issue
kpi_monitoring: Pre-fix baseline: NTM_NR_PWR_03_15_010 (PUSCH Interference), NTM_NR_CAP_03_04_061 (DL Throughput)
Post-fix: PUSCH Interference should return to -110 to -120 dBm range within 1 ROP (15 min)
Post-fix: DL throughput should recover to baseline within 24h

mitigation_playbooks.title: RSSI Branch Imbalance Playbook
field_actions: Step 1: AMOS: pmx (live per-branch RSSI for |Rx0-Rx1| delta); get RfBranch=* ulAttenuation
Step 2: Identify which branch is degraded (lower RSSI = suspected path)
Step 3: Use tone generator at DAS head-end, trace signal through each path segment
Step 4: Common culprits: corroded N-type connectors at antenna, disconnected jumper at splitter, failed remote unit port
Step 5: Inspect DAS antenna port connectivity, splitter outputs, jumper cables, RU ports systematically
Step 6: Post-fix: verify |Rx0 - Rx1| < 3 dB on all bands
parameter_actions: Verify CIQ noOfTxAntennas and noOfRxAntennas match actual physical config
kpi_monitoring: ClosedLoop_2x2MIMORank2Usage should recover to > 60%
DL Average UE Throughput should increase up to 30% if Rank 2 is restored
Check pmRadioTxRankDistr — Rank 2+ should exceed 60%

mitigation_playbooks.title: Fiber Loss / Fronthaul Fault Playbook
field_actions: Step 1: AMOS: get SfpModule=* rxPower — verify both RXdBm1 and RXdBm2 > -5 dBm
Step 2: OTDR test on fiber run to locate fault point (bend, break, dirty connector)
Step 3: Clean all fiber connectors with IPA wipes and fiber inspection scope
Step 4: If single-direction loss: check SFP transceiver on affected end, replace if degraded
Step 5: If BiDi SFP: verify TX/RX wavelength pairing is correct (common JMA TEKO issue)
Step 6: Post-fix: verify fiber loss within +3 to -3 dB range, Rx > -5 dBm on both ends
parameter_actions: None for fiber — physical fix. Check eCPRI configuration if packet-based fronthaul
kpi_monitoring: NR Cell Availability (NTM_NR_AVL_03_08_001) should be 100%
BLER should return to 5-10% optimal range
NR_DL_Scheduling should recover to 65-75% (NSA) or 85-100% (SA)

mitigation_playbooks.title: RTWP / UL Interference Playbook
field_actions: Step 1: AMOS: invfrx (live per-sector RTWP) — compare across sectors. Single sector elevated = cable/PIM. All sectors = external
Step 2: If single sector: PIM sweep first (most common cause in DAS)
Step 3: If all sectors: external interference scan with spectrum analyzer
Step 4: Check antenna isolation between sectors/technologies
Step 5: Check DAS amplifier input power vs. rated max — over-driven amps generate broadband noise
Step 6: Verify DAS cascaded noise figure < 6 dB
parameter_actions: If RTWP persists after PIM fix: adjust PRACH config — increase numPreambles, preambleTransMax
Consider increasing prach-ConfigIndex, add RACH resources
Adjust powerRampingStep for UL power control optimization
kpi_monitoring: NTM_NR_PWR_03_15_010 (PUSCH Interference) should return below -105 dBm
RACH CBRA SR should recover to > 99% in DAS
PMRADIORACBFAILMSG1OOC should drop to zero
UE TX Power should return to always-negative range

mitigation_playbooks.title: PIM Remediation Playbook
field_actions: Step 1: PIM sweep with analyzer — test both forward and reverse intermod
Step 2: Replace ALL connectors showing corrosion, weathering, or discoloration
Step 3: Torque all connections to manufacturer spec (15-20 ft-lbs for N-type)
Step 4: Replace jumper cables older than 5 years
Step 5: Verify no dissimilar metals in RF path (brass-to-stainless contact = PIM source)
Step 6: For DAS: check headend-to-remote distribution connectors specifically
Step 7: Post-fix: PIM test should confirm < -153 dBc on all paths
parameter_actions: None — PIM is physical. No parameter change will fix PIM.
kpi_monitoring: SINR should recover by 3-10 dB (the PIM degradation range)
PUSCH Interference Power should normalize to -110 to -120 dBm
DL Average UE Throughput should increase proportionally to SINR recovery
NSA SN Total Drop Rate should decrease as sustained low SINR events reduce

mitigation_playbooks.title: GPS / Synchronization Loss Playbook
field_actions: Step 1: AMOS: get RadioEquipmentClock=* radioClockState — verify GPS lock status
Step 2: Check GPS antenna cable for damage, verify sky visibility
Step 3: Verify SyncE / IEEE 1588v2 PTP configuration on transport equipment
Step 4: For TDD (n41): sync loss = DL/UL collision = immediate service impact
Step 5: Check Router 6675 / Fronthaul 6000 sync status
parameter_actions: Verify tddUlDlConfig matches planned TDD pattern (DDDSU for T-Mobile n41)
Check guard period configuration — 1 GP symbol per ~550m cell radius
kpi_monitoring: NR Cell Availability — GPS loss may cause cell-level outage
BLER — should spike massively if TDD timing is misaligned
DL/UL Throughput — near-zero during sync loss

mitigation_playbooks.title: EN-DC PLMN / MOCN Misconfiguration Playbook
field_actions: Step 1: AMOS: GET EUtranCellFDD=* endcAllowedPlmnList — verify ONLY 310/260 present
Step 2: If 311/490 found → Script 3 execution was incomplete — re-run
Step 3: GET EUtranCellFDD=[L|B|F].*,UeMeasControl=1 nrB1MeasEnabled — verify True on all L/B/F cells
Step 4: GET GUtranFreqRelation=* connectedModeMobilityPrio — verify -1 for all except N25 1C (should be 7)
Step 5: lget Lm featureState|licenseState | grep CXC4012324 — if licenseState=ENABLED but featureState=DEACTIVATED, ACTIVATE
parameter_actions: Re-execute Script 3 (MOCN_Deletion_RWR_activation_v6) if PLMN audit fails
Verify Yellow Carrier logic: if PLMN 312/250 present, preserve it while removing 311/490
kpi_monitoring: NSA SN Addition Failure Rate should drop below 1% (venue) / 0.5% (macro)
ERAB Drop Rate should normalize if PLMN conflicts were causing bearer drops
LTE ENDC Setup Failures should approach 0%

mitigation_playbooks.title: SA Mobility / Drop Rate Playbook (A5 & rsrpCritical)
field_actions: Step 1: GET McpcPCellProfile=*,McpcPCellProfileUeCfg=Base rsrpCandidateA5 — verify threshold pair
Step 2: GET McpcPCellProfile=*,McpcPCellProfileUeCfg=Base rsrpCriticalEnabled — MUST be true
Step 3: Verify correct script variant applied: Script 12 for SA 2C/80MHz, Script 13 for SA 2C/<80MHz
Step 4: Check TTT: Base=320ms (macro appropriate), Venue may need verification
Step 5: Analyze ping-pong: if HO attempts high but SR low → TTT too short or hysteresis too low
parameter_actions: Sites with SA drop > 0.5%: consider tightening A5.threshold1 by 3-5 dB (from -115 to -112/-110)
Verify rsrpCritical thresholds: base=-115, VoNR A.*=-112, VoNR J.*/I.*=-119
If VoNR B2 threshold2EUtra is not -125: VoNR users may attempt inter-RAT fallback (bad in SA)
Check filterCoefficientNrRsrpHigh/Low=4 for L3 smoothing; filterCoefficientNrRsrqHigh/Low=11
kpi_monitoring: SA HO Intra-Freq SR: target > 90% venue, > 95% macro
SA NGBR Data DRB Drop Rate: target < 2% venue, < 0.5% macro
PSCell Change SR: target > 99% macro
Compare Prep Failures (NTM_NR_MOB_03_05_107) vs Exec Failures (NTM_NR_MOB_03_05_111)

mitigation_playbooks.title: RSSI Hot / DAS Over-Driving Playbook
field_actions: Step 1: AMOS: pmx (live per-cell RSSI); invfrx (live UL interference) — identify all bands with RSSI above hot threshold
Step 2: If ALL bands hot simultaneously: DAS amplifier saturation — check headend output power
Step 3: If SINGLE band hot: carrier-specific gain issue — check per-band DAS gain settings
Step 4: Measure DAS headend composite output power — should be 10+ dB below remote unit P1dB
Step 5: If AILG available: enable AILG to auto-attenuate (target PCS/AWS: -114 to -115 dBm; N2500: -110 to -112 dBm)
Step 6: If no AILG: manually reduce headend output or add fixed attenuator at RRU input
Step 7: Post-fix: verify RSSI within passing range, PIM sweep to confirm no PIM generated
parameter_actions: Enable AILG if supported on affected bands
Adjust p0NominalPUSCH if RSSI Hot is causing UL interference (reduce target)
kpi_monitoring: PUSCH Interference should normalize to -110 to -120 dBm after power reduction
SINR should improve if amplifier distortion was degrading quality
BLER should return to 5-10% optimal if EVM was issue

mitigation_playbooks.title: RSSI Cold / Dead DAS Path Playbook
field_actions: Step 1: AMOS: pmx (live per-cell RSSI); invfrx (live UL interference) — identify bands below cold threshold
Step 2: If ALL bands cold: DAS remote unit failure or complete path disconnect
Step 3: If SINGLE band cold: band-specific amplifier or filter failure in DAS
Step 4: Trace UL path from antenna → splitter → remote unit → fiber → headend → RRU
Step 5: Inject tone at antenna port, measure at each node to isolate failure point
Step 6: Check DAS remote unit LED status, power supply, fuse status
Step 7: Post-fix: verify RSSI within passing range on all bands
parameter_actions: If persistent cold on specific band: increase preambleTransMax and powerRampingStep to improve UL reliability
Consider increasing msg3PowerOffsetGroupB for better UL access on weak paths
kpi_monitoring: RACH CBRA SR should recover to > 99% once UL path restored
Msg1 OOC failures should drop to zero
UE TX Power should return to always-negative range

mitigation_playbooks.title: Transport / Backhaul Fault Playbook
field_actions: Step 1: AMOS: check pmTransportBhLatency — if > 20ms, backhaul congestion or routing issue
Step 2: AMOS: check pmSctpAssocFail — if non-zero, S1/NG interface transport issue
Step 3: For eCPRI fronthaul: check pmEcpriSyncLoss and pmEcpriPktLoss
Step 4: Verify backhaul link capacity vs. aggregate cell throughput — ensure no BH bottleneck
Step 5: Check Router 6675 / Fronthaul 6000 interface errors and CRC counts
Step 6: For JMA/TEKO DAS: verify backhaul fiber between DAS gateway and BBU is clean
Step 7: If SCTP failure: verify IP addressing, VLAN tagging, and firewall rules to MME/AMF
parameter_actions: If packet loss detected: review RLC t-Reassembly timer — increase from default if jitter is high
If latency > 20ms: check QoS marking and DSCP priority on transport network
Verify eCPRI compression mode matches both ends of fronthaul link
kpi_monitoring: NR Cell Availability should recover to > 99.9%
DL/UL Throughput should normalize once BH congestion resolved
RLC Max Retransmission events should drop to near-zero
SA NGBR Drop Rate should normalize as transport stabilizes

mitigation_playbooks.title: Radio Unit Hardware Fault Playbook
field_actions: Step 1: AMOS: hget FieldReplaceableUnit=* operationalState|hwModel — identify specific RU and degradation type
Step 2: AMOS: hget . bbLinkStatus (fronthaul only) — check BB-RU link (fiber or cable)
Step 3: AMOS: st Radio (sector-level state) — identify which specific RF ports are affected
Step 4: If single port: verify jumper and antenna connection on that port
Step 5: If all ports: power cycle RU via AMOS (if remote power control available)
Step 6: If power cycle fails: dispatch field crew for physical inspection
Step 7: Check RU firmware version — known bugs may require SW update
Step 8: Replace RU if hardware fault confirmed — maintain same RfPort→antenna mapping
parameter_actions: None — hardware fault requires physical fix
Post-replacement: verify CIQ parameters match (frequencyDL/UL, bSChannelBwDL/UL, configuredMaxTxPower)
kpi_monitoring: NR Cell Availability should return to 100% once RU restored
DL/UL Throughput on affected cells should normalize
Verify RSSI, VSWR, and fiber loss all within spec after replacement

mitigation_playbooks.title: DAS Amplifier Saturation / Oscillation Playbook
field_actions: Step 1: Confirm saturation: ALL bands show RSSI Hot simultaneously = amplifier-level issue, not band-specific
Step 2: Measure composite DAS headend output power with power meter
Step 3: Compare measured power to DAS remote unit P1dB specification — must be 10+ dB below P1dB
Step 4: Calculate cascaded noise figure — should be < 6 dB
Step 5: If over-driven: reduce headend composite output by 3-6 dB
Step 6: If oscillation suspected: check for feedback loops in DAS design, verify isolation between input/output
Step 7: For multi-operator DAS: verify total composite power across all operators below spec
parameter_actions: Enable AILG (Automatic Idle Link Gain) on affected bands if not already enabled
If AILG not available: manually adjust per-carrier power sharing in RRU to reduce composite power
kpi_monitoring: RTWP should normalize to -110 to -120 dBm range
PUSCH Interference should drop below -105 dBm
SINR should recover as broadband noise eliminated
BLER should return to optimal 5-10% range

mitigation_playbooks.title: Missing Feature License / Activation Playbook
field_actions: Step 1: AMOS: lget Lm featureState|licenseState|description — list all features
Step 2: Filter: licenseState=ENABLED, featureState=DEACTIVATED — these are licensed but not activated
Step 3: Priority check: CXC4012324 (EN-DC) — if featureState=DEACTIVATED, EN-DC is blocked on this cell
Step 4: For CA license: verify NR_CA and LTE_CA features both activated
Step 5: Activate missing features: SET FeatureState=CXC4012324 featureState=1
Step 6: For VoNR: verify both ims-related features AND epsFallbackOp=3 are configured
parameter_actions: After feature activation: verify dependent parameters are configured (e.g., EN-DC activation also requires b1ThresholdRsrp, endcAllowedPlmnList)
Run relevant PAG script to configure parameters for newly activated features
kpi_monitoring: NSA SN Addition FR should drop as EN-DC becomes available
EN-DC Setup SR should increase from 0% to target (>85% venue, >90% macro)
DL Throughput should increase if CA was newly activated

mitigation_playbooks.name: NR-DC Setup Failure Troubleshooting Playbook

steps.action: Verify NR-DC is enabled on all cells
steps.expected: nrDcAllowed=true on all cells that should support NR-DC
steps.if_fail: Set nrDcAllowed=true on affected cells. If intentionally disabled, document reason.

steps.action: Check NR-DC band combinations
steps.expected: All required band pairs listed (e.g., N19+N258, N25+N258 for Dodger Stadium)
steps.if_fail: Add missing band combinations via AMOS set command

steps.action: Verify Xn/TermPointToGNB between MN and SN nodes
steps.amos: get ExternalGNBCUCPFunction=* gNBId administrativeState; get TermPointToGNB=* ipAddress
steps.expected: Bidirectional ExternalGNBCUCPFunction with matching gNBIds, TermPointToGNB with valid IP, adminState=UNLOCKED
steps.if_fail: Check IX relation scripts, verify gNBId consistency with CIQ, confirm Xn transport path

steps.action: Verify xnIpAddrViaNgActive post-IX state
steps.expected: xnIpAddrViaNgActive=true (should be re-enabled after IX relation creation)

steps.action: Check NRCellRelation DC parameters
steps.expected: caStatus allows DC, isHoAllowed=true on NR-DC candidate relations, appropriate CIO values
steps.if_fail: Review relation parameters per PAG guidance, ensure DC-candidate relations have correct flags

steps.action: Check NR-DC license feature
steps.if_fail: Escalate to NPI for license activation

steps.action: Compare MN vs SN setup counters
steps.analysis: If MN attempts >> SN attempts → Xn signaling failing (MN sends request but SN never receives). If SN attempts == MN attempts but SN success << SN attempts → SN resource allocation failing (mmWave cell overloaded or misconfigured).

mitigation_playbooks.escalation: If all steps pass but NR-DC SR still low, collect XnAP traces and escalate to Ericsson TAC with pmNrdcSetup counter trends
mitigation_playbooks.dodger_stadium_notes: Check all 3 sites (LAY5501A/5502A/5503A) independently. Cross-site NR-DC (e.g., MLAY5501A → NLAY5502A) requires inter-site Xn which may need separate validation.

mitigation_playbooks.name: SCG Failure / High NR-DC Drop Rate Playbook

steps.action: Identify dominant SCG failure cause
steps.analysis: Calculate percentage breakdown: beam_failure%, rlc_retx%, t310%, rach_failure%, other%

steps.action: If beam failure dominant — check mmWave coverage and CPC
steps.remediation: Enable CPC if disabled. Adjust cpcThresholdRsrp. Check active antenna tilt/orientation. Verify beam management parameters.

steps.action: If t310 dominant — tune SCG timers
steps.remediation: Increase t310 from 1000ms to 2000ms for mmWave venues. Increase n310 count. This tolerates brief blockage events (human body, rotation).

steps.action: If RLC retx dominant — check SCG bearer quality
steps.remediation: Check SINR/CQI on mmWave cells. High RLC retx with good SINR → check scheduling. Low SINR → coverage issue.

steps.action: Check abnormal release distribution (active vs inactive)
steps.analysis: High Act/non-Act ratio = failures during active data transfer (more user-impacting). Low ratio = failures during idle DC (less impacting).

mitigation_playbooks.escalation: If SCG failure rate > 40% after timer tuning and CPC enablement, collect L3 UE logs during NR-DC failure events and escalate

mitigation_playbooks.name: mmWave Venue NR-DC Optimization Playbook (Multi-Site DAS)
mitigation_playbooks.trigger_kpi: NR-DC performance issues at multi-site mmWave DAS venue
mitigation_playbooks.venue_applicability: Stadiums, arenas, convention centers with 3-node architecture (MLAY+NLAY+NLAY2)

steps.action: Validate 3-node architecture cross-references
steps.check: For each site: verify MLAY↔NLAY, MLAY↔NLAY2, NLAY2↔NLAY all have bidirectional ExternalGNBCUCPFunction + TermPointToGNB

steps.action: Validate cross-site Xn for seam zone NR-DC
steps.check: At venue boundaries (e.g., DAS 1 ↔ DAS 2 seam), verify inter-site ExternalGNBCUCPFunction exists (MLAY5501A ↔ NLAY5502A)
steps.note: Inter-site NR-DC requires explicit Xn configuration in IX relation scripts

steps.action: Verify PCI plan for NR-DC PSCell selection
steps.check: mmWave PCIs (570-572, 600-602, 606-607) must be unique within NR-DC reselection range. PCI collision between sites causes PSCell selection ambiguity.

steps.action: Check NR-DC performance per site pair
steps.amos: pget ^nrcell pmNrdcSetupUeMnAtt$|pmNrdcSetupUeMnSucc$ (run on each MLAY/NLAY2 node)
steps.analysis: Compare NR-DC SR across sites. If one site has significantly lower SR, issue is site-specific (hardware, config). If all sites similar, issue is venue-wide (band combo, license, timer).

steps.action: Assess crowd impact on mmWave NR-DC
steps.method: Compare NR-DC counters during event vs. non-event periods. High crowd density causes: (a) more beam blockage → higher SCG failure rate, (b) more NR-DC attempts due to capacity steering → lower SR if resources constrained.
steps.recommendation: If event-driven degradation > 30%, consider: increasing mmWave sectors, adjusting beam tilt for crowd-level coverage, enabling CPC with aggressive thresholds.

mitigation_playbooks.name: SN Change Immediate Collapse Playbook (mmWave PSCell Mobility)
mitigation_playbooks.trigger_kpi: NTM NR RET SA NRDC SN SN Total Drop Rate elevated, with K_NRDC_SETUP_SR normal (setup works, retention fails)
mitigation_playbooks.trigger_pattern: NR-DC setup succeeds but SCG collapses within <100ms — visible in protocol trace as rrcReconfigComplete followed immediately by UEContextRelease

steps.action: Confirm SN Change vs SN Addition
steps.method: Decode XnAP messages — look for SgNBChangeRequired (SN Change) vs SgNBAdditionRequest (SN Addition). If SN Change: this playbook applies. If SN Addition: use PB_NRDC_SETUP_FAILURE instead.
steps.why: Failure attribution and parameter tuning are completely different for SN Change vs SN Addition

steps.action: Check pmNrScgFailureCause on the NEW SN
steps.amos: pget ^nrcell pmNrScgFailureCause* (run on the target/new SN cells, NOT the source/old SN)
steps.why: In SN Change context, failure attribution shifts to the new SN. Checking old SN gives misleading results.
steps.analysis: Beam failure cause → Root Cause #1. RLC cause → Root Cause #3 (PDCP). Timer cause → Root Cause #4.

steps.action: Decode the triggering MeasReport — event type and RSRP
steps.method: In protocol trace, find the MeasReport that triggered SN Change. Check: (a) event type (A3 offset-based or A5 threshold-based), (b) measured RSRP of new SN cell, (c) RSRP of old SN cell at that moment.
steps.analysis: If new SN RSRP < -100 dBm at trigger → A3 offset too small, cell was marginal at selection. If old SN RSRP still acceptable → SN Change was premature.
steps.fix: Increase a3Offset (from 3dB to 6dB for mmWave). Increase timeToTriggerA3 (from 160ms to 320ms+ for mmWave).

steps.action: Check RA duration and beam sweep on new SN
steps.amos: get NRSectorCarrier=* ssbPeriodicity|beamSweepTimer*; get NRCellDU=* ssbPeriodicity
steps.analysis: 200ms RA = multiple preamble retries = weak beam. Normal mmWave RA should complete in 20-50ms with good beam.
steps.fix: If ssbPeriodicity > 20ms on mmWave → reduce to 10-20ms for denser beam sweeps. Check if beamSweepTimerDuration allows enough sweep attempts.

steps.action: Review tSCGconfigTimer on MN
steps.analysis: If timer < 500ms → too aggressive for mmWave SN Change where RA alone takes 200ms.
steps.fix: Increase tSCGconfigTimer to 500-1000ms for mmWave venue. This gives the new SCG time to: complete RA (200ms) + beam lock (50-100ms) + first scheduling (50ms) + PDCP sync (50ms).
steps.risk: Setting too high delays detection of genuinely failed SN Changes — 1000ms is practical upper bound.

steps.action: Verify PDCP forwarding completeness
steps.method: Decode XnAP trace — check SNStatusTransfer contents in setup vs any SNStatusTransfer in teardown. Different SN counts = incomplete forwarding.
steps.fix: If PDCP forwarding race condition confirmed → escalate to Ericsson TAC as potential software defect in SN Change PDCP handling on BB6648.

steps.action: Enable CPC as preventive measure
steps.fix: CPC pre-evaluates candidate PSCells, filtering out marginal cells before SN Change triggers. This prevents Root Causes #1 and #2 by ensuring the SN Change target is viable.
steps.expected_improvement: 30-50% reduction in SN Change failures by avoiding marginal PSCell targets

mitigation_playbooks.escalation: If Steps 3-5 tuned and SN Change collapse persists with <50ms windows, collect XnAP+RRC traces and escalate as potential BB6648 firmware issue with tSCGconfigTimer behavior.
mitigation_playbooks.ntm_kpi_mapping: NTM NR RET SA NRDC SN SN Total Drop Rate

mitigation_playbooks.name: NR-DC SN Baseline Parameter Audit
mitigation_playbooks.trigger: NR-DC retainability failure on mmWave SN after NRDC activation scripts executed
mitigation_playbooks.description: Systematic audit of SN-side parameters that the FR2 activation script does not configure. These baseline parameters from the venue template directly impact SCG stability during NR-DC.

steps.action: Check CG Switch configuration on all SN cells
steps.if_mismatch: set dlCgSwitchMode 0 on all CgSwitchCfg MOs — CG switching is destructive for NR-DC SCG stability

steps.action: Check t310 RLF timer on SN
steps.if_mismatch: set t310 6000 — mmWave beam recovery needs ≥6s window

steps.action: Check HARQ mode on SN
steps.if_mismatch: set dlHarqMode 2 — robust HARQ essential for mmWave burst error recovery

steps.action: Check rsrpCritical monitoring on PSCell and PCell profiles
steps.if_mismatch: Enable rsrpCritical on all PSCell/PCell profiles. This is the single most important parameter — without it, MN cannot proactively manage degrading PSCells

steps.action: Check MCPC PCell/PSCell enable flags
steps.if_mismatch: set mcpcPCellEnabled true and mcpcPSCellEnabled true on all cells

steps.action: Check DC DL aggregation CG priority
steps.if_mismatch: set dcDlAggCgPrio 1 — prioritize SCG for DL to leverage mmWave capacity

steps.action: Check SSB beam configuration
steps.expected: Evaluate if SSB_BEAMS_24 is necessary; SSB_BEAMS_12 provides better stability for NR-DC
steps.if_mismatch: Discuss with RF team — reducing from 24 to 12 SSB beams trades peak beam gain for stability

steps.action: Check CA measurement thresholds
steps.expected: rsrpBetterSCell: hysteresis≥100, offset≥100, TTT≥1280. rsrpSCellCoverage threshold≥-120
steps.if_mismatch: Align CA meas to reference values — aggressive thresholds create SCell churn that destabilizes NR-DC SCG

steps.action: Verify FR1 MN-side NR-DC parameters
steps.expected: nrdcEnabled=true, rsrpPSCellCandidate threshold=-113, McpcNrdcPSCell rsrpCritical threshold=-119, nrdcSnTermAllowed=32767

mitigation_playbooks.name: FR2 NRDC Activation Script Enhancement
mitigation_playbooks.trigger: New NR-DC venue deployment or existing venue showing NR-DC retainability issues
mitigation_playbooks.description: The standard FR2 script (v3) only activates licenses. This playbook defines the SN-side parameter additions needed in the FR2 script for NR-DC stability.

steps.action: Add CG Switch disable to FR2 script
steps.mos_addition:
set GNBDUFunction=1,UeCC=1,CgSwitchCfg=.* dlCgSwitchMode 0
set GNBDUFunction=1,UeCC=1,CgSwitchCfg=.* dlScgLowQualThresh -400
steps.rationale: Prevent constant MCG↔SCG switching that destabilizes NR-DC

steps.action: Add t310 increase to FR2 script
steps.rationale: Give mmWave SN 6 seconds for beam recovery before RLF

steps.action: Add HARQ robust mode to FR2 script
steps.rationale: Robust HARQ for mmWave burst error resilience

steps.action: Add rsrpCritical enable to FR2 script
steps.mos_addition:
set Mcpc=1,McpcPSCellProfile=Default,McpcPSCellProfileUeCfg=Base rsrpCriticalEnabled true
set Mcpc=1,McpcPSCellProfile=Default,McpcPSCellProfileUeCfg=Base rsrpCritical hysteresis=10,threshold=-112,timeToTrigger=160
set Mcpc=1,McpcPSCellProfile=1,McpcPSCellProfileUeCfg=Base rsrpCriticalEnabled true
set Mcpc=1,McpcPSCellProfile=1,McpcPSCellProfileUeCfg=Base rsrpCritical hysteresis=10,threshold=-112,timeToTrigger=160
steps.rationale: Enable proactive PSCell degradation detection — most critical missing parameter

steps.action: Add MCPC enable to FR2 script
steps.mos_addition:
set NRCellCU=.* mcpcPCellEnabled true
set NRCellCU=.* mcpcPSCellEnabled true
steps.rationale: Enable multi-panel beam coordination for NR-DC PSCell

steps.action: Add DC DL priority to FR2 script

mitigation_playbooks.scope: All mmWave NR-DC SN nodes in venue

mitigation_playbooks.name: Multi-gNB Venue Pre-Integration Validation
mitigation_playbooks.description: Validate all cross-gNB configuration consistency before integration of a multi-node venue.
mitigation_playbooks.trigger: New venue IX with 2+ gNBs sharing a TAC
steps: 1. Verify all gNBs share the same TAC (trackingAreaCode must be identical)
2. Validate PCI plan — no duplicates within venue, min separation of 3 from adjacent cells
3. Confirm RACH root sequence uniqueness across all sectors in the venue
4. Verify CORENET VLAN assignment — shared where appropriate, isolated where needed
5. Confirm all AMF termpoints configured identically across gNBs (same 16 AMFs)
6. Validate SSB frequency offsets — no overlap between co-located sectors
7. Cross-check CIQ exports against integration scripts for parameter consistency
8. Verify VSWR supervision enabled on all RF ports (CXC4012024, sensitivity=100)
9. Confirm external alarm ports configured per venue alarm panel wiring
10. Run ATND acceptance test on each gNB independently, then validate cross-gNB handover

mitigation_playbooks.name: Venue PCI Collision Audit
mitigation_playbooks.description: Systematic PCI collision check for multi-gNB venue deployments.
mitigation_playbooks.trigger: New venue integration or PCI-related handover failures
steps: 1. Extract all PCIs from integration scripts (NRCellDU physicalCellIdentity)
2. Build PCI map: gNB → sector → PCI for entire venue
3. Check for duplicates within venue (zero tolerance)
4. Check for mod-3 collisions (same PCI mod 3 in adjacent sectors causes SSS confusion)
5. Validate against neighboring macro site PCIs (scanner data or ENM neighbor dump)
6. Verify RACH root sequences don't create cross-sector false detections
7. If collision found: reassign using sequential blocks with gaps for expansion
