cross_domain_chains.name: IX Integration Sequence — Hardware to Relations
cross_domain_chains.description: The 52-script integration sequence reveals the causal dependency chain in site deployment: Transport must precede RAN, RAN must precede Radio Hardware, Hardware must precede Cell Config, Cell Config must precede Relations. Any failure in an upstream phase cascades to all downstream phases.
cross_domain_chains.governing_insight: The relation-scripting phase (Steps 30-52) accounts for 88% of all parameters (3,302/3,752), making it the highest-risk phase for misconfig. A single PCI collision or missing frequency relation can cascade to HO failures (T4) and drop rate KPI degradation (T5).

cross_domain_chains.name: mmWave Stadium Venue Causal Chain
cross_domain_chains.description: Cross-domain chain specific to mmWave DAS venue deployments (N258/N260). Captures unique causal relationships: active antenna thermal→beam quality, SCS=120kHz measurement timing→handover delay, multi-node architecture (MLAY+NLAY+NLAY2)→cross-node handover complexity, high cell density (76 cells/venue)→PCI/PRACH planning criticality.

chain.detail: Active antenna (AIR5331/AIR5322) thermal alarm or power supply fault
mmWave RSRP drops sharply due to high free-space path loss (>20dB/100m at 24GHz)
SINR degradation from inter-channel interference across 5 N258 channels within site
A5 threshold triggers PCell change, but mmWave beam alignment latency adds delay
VoNR fallback to LTE VoLTE when mmWave coverage lost behind stadium structure
Cross-node handover NLAY→MLAY requires X2/Xn setup across BB6648 and RP6651
Elevated CSFB/fallback rate in mmWave-only coverage zones during high-attendance events

cross_domain_chains.key_insight: mmWave venues have 3-5x more cells than sub-6 equivalents (76 vs 15), creating exponentially more relation permutations (1,116 freq relations). Integration sequence complexity scales accordingly.

cross_domain_chains.name: NR-DC Setup Failure — End-to-End Causal Chain
cross_domain_chains.description: Traces NR-DC setup failure from configuration through counters to KPI and user impact

steps.action: nrDcAllowed=false OR missing band combo OR broken Xn
steps.description: NR-DC configuration prerequisites: nrdcAllowed, band combo support (T3_NRDC_BAND_COMBO), and Xn termpoint (T3_XN_TERMPOINT) must all be correctly configured

steps.action: Xn setup fails between MN and SN gNBs (pmXnSetupFail)
NR-DC setup attempts fail (pmNrdcSetupUeMnSucc << pmNrdcSetupUeMnAtt)
NR-DC Setup SR drops below 85% threshold
UE throughput limited to sub-6 only (~200-500 Mbps vs 1-4 Gbps with mmWave DC)
Stadium users experience poor throughput despite mmWave infrastructure investment
steps.description: End user experiences reduced throughput — stuck on single-carrier NR SA instead of NR-DC dual connectivity

cross_domain_chains.dodger_stadium_relevance: Primary chain for diagnosing why NR-DC calls fail at the stadium. Start at T5 (low NR-DC SR), trace back through T4 (which counter is failing), to T3 (which configuration is wrong).

cross_domain_chains.name: mmWave Beam Failure → SCG Drop → Throughput Cliff Chain
cross_domain_chains.description: Traces mmWave beam failure through SCG handling to throughput impact — the NR-DC retainability chain

steps.action: mmWave signal blocked by human body, object, or antenna thermal derating
steps.description: mmWave beam blockage (hand/body/object) or active antenna thermal throttling causes sudden signal degradation on SCG leg

steps.action: Beam failure detected on PSCell (consecutive PDCCH missed)
t310 timer starts on SCG. n310 out-of-sync indications accumulate.
t310 expires → pmScgFailureT310Expiry or pmScgFailureBeamFailure increments
MN-initiated abnormal release on SN side (pmNrdcRelUeSnAbnormalMnInit)
SCG failure rate and abnormal release rate exceed thresholds
steps.description: SCG failure rate increases; also impacts NR-DC abnormal release rate (K_NRDC_ABNORMAL_REL_RATE)

steps.action: Throughput drops from ~1-4 Gbps (NR-DC active) to ~200-500 Mbps (sub-6 SA only)

cross_domain_chains.mitigation_path: Enable CPC (T3_CPC) → UE pre-switches PSCell before beam loss → avoids SCG failure → throughput cliff avoided

cross_domain_chains.name: Cross-Node Relation Error → Silent NR-DC Block Chain
cross_domain_chains.description: The most insidious NR-DC failure: misconfigured cross-node references silently prevent NR-DC with zero alarms

steps.action: ExternalGNBCUCPFunction gNBId mismatch OR xnIpAddrViaNgActive left false post-IX
steps.description: Misconfigured Xn termpoint or disabled XnIP toggle (T3_XNIP_TOGGLE) silently blocks NR-DC setup path

steps.action: NRCellRelation caStatus wrong or isHoAllowed=false on cross-node relations
Xn never establishes between affected gNBs (pmXnSetupReq may show 0)
pmNrdcSetupUeMnAtt=0 on affected cells (NR-DC never even attempted — not a 'failure', just never starts)
Zero alarms generated. NR-DC simply doesn't exist on this node pair. Only detectable by checking counters.
steps.description: NR-DC setup counter stays at zero — no alarm is raised because the feature is licensed but the signaling path is broken. The absence of attempts (not failures) makes this invisible to standard KPI monitoring.

steps.action: mmWave capacity stranded — investment in AIR5331/AIR5322 antennas yields zero NR-DC benefit on affected sectors
steps.description: Silent capacity loss — venue UEs never get NR-DC, throughput stays at single-carrier levels with no alarm or counter indicating the gap

cross_domain_chains.detection_method: Run pget pmNrdcSetupUeMnAtt on all MLAY/NLAY2 cells. Any cell with Att=0 while adjacent cells have Att>0 → investigate Xn/relation config for that cell's node pair.

cross_domain_chains.name: SN Change → Immediate SCG Collapse → Retainability Failure Chain
cross_domain_chains.description: Traces the Dodger Stadium NR-DC retainability failure: SN Change completes at signaling layer but new SCG collapses within milliseconds. Distinct from SN Addition failure (accessibility) — this is PSCell mobility failure (retainability).

steps.action: A3/A5 measurement triggers SN Change to new mmWave PSCell — but measured RSRP is marginal (too close to release threshold)
UE attempts RA on new SN — takes 200ms with multiple preamble retries indicating weak beam at new PSCell
MN forwards PDCP SN status from old SN to new SN — SNStatusTransfer may not complete cleanly (T3_PDCP_SN_CHANGE)
steps.description: XnAP SNStatusTransfer carries PDCP state from source SN to target SN for lossless bearer continuity

steps.action: SN Change completes — NR-DC is technically established on new PSCell (pmNrScgChangeSuccess increments)
steps.description: UE sends rrcReconfigurationComplete confirming new SCG config applied — NR-DC SN Change signaling completes successfully

steps.action: New SN beam fails immediately OR tSCGconfigTimer fires (15ms window) — MN decides new SCG is not viable
steps.description: Post-reconfig, tSCG config timer expires or beam failure occurs before SCG data path stabilizes — 14ms collapse window observed at LAH0050A

steps.action: MN sends UEContextRelease to new SN — pmNrScgChangeFail and pmNrdcRelUeSnAbnormalMnInit increment
steps.description: SCG change failure counter increments; also triggers NR-DC release (T4_NRDC_RELEASE) as the dual connectivity session tears down

steps.action: SN Change SR degrades, NTM retainability KPI pegs — visible as 'NR-DC call failure' in venue KPI dashboards
steps.description: SN Change success rate degrades; maps to NTM NR RET SA NRDC SN SN Total Drop Rate KPI in T-Mobile dashboards

diagnostic_actions: 1. Decode XnAP: SgNBAdditionRequest vs SgNBChangeRequired — confirm SN Change not SN Addition
2. Check pmNrScgFailureCause on NEW SN (not old SN) — failure attribution shifts in SN Change context
3. Decode MeasReport at :316 — extract event type (A3/A5) and measured RSRP of new SN cell
4. Review 200ms RA duration — check beamSweepTimerDuration and SSB periodicity on new SN
5. Review tSCGconfigTimer on MN — if too aggressive post-SN Change, will trigger immediate release

cross_domain_chains.name: NR-DC SN Baseline Parameter Mismatch → SCG Retainability Failure
cross_domain_chains.description: The FR2 activation script enables NR-DC licenses but does not tune SN-side parameters. The venue's aggressive mmWave baseline (optimized for standalone throughput) is incompatible with NR-DC SCG stability requirements.

chain.description: FR2 script activates 4 licenses (CXC4012582, CXC4012315, CXC4012273, CXC4012375) but configures ZERO parameters
Venue baseline dlCgSwitchMode=ENABLED with dlScgLowQualThresh=50 persists → constant CG switching
Venue baseline t310=2000ms persists → aggressive RLF timer on mmWave SN
Venue baseline rsrpCriticalEnabled=false persists → no proactive PSCell degradation detection
Venue baseline HARQ_NORMAL persists → less resilient to mmWave burst errors
SCG failures spike: beam failure → t310 expires in 2s → RLF → SCG release. No preemptive PSCell change because rsrpCritical disabled
SN Changes fail: new PSCell has same aggressive baseline → immediate collapse (14ms pattern from protocol trace)
NTM NR RET SA NRDC SN SN Total Drop Rate spikes across all sectors — template-level issue, not cell-specific

evidence.diffo_source: mmwave LAH0050A vs LAD100BA.xlsx — 579 parameter differences on mmWave node
evidence.ntm_kpi: NR-DC SN drop rate elevated on LAY5501A, LAD1008A, LA02744X (all venues)

cross_domain_chains.resolution: Run PB_SN_BASELINE_AUDIT on all SN nodes, then enhance FR2 script per PB_FR2_SCRIPT_ENHANCEMENT

cross_domain_chains.name: FR2 License-Only Script → Systematic NR-DC Venue Failure
cross_domain_chains.description: The structural gap between FR1 (comprehensive MN config) and FR2 (license-only SN activation) creates a systematic NR-DC failure pattern at every venue where the scripts are deployed.

chain.description: FR1 correctly configures MN: nrdcEnabled=true, rsrpPSCellCandidate=-113/TTT=320, rsrpCritical=-119/TTT=160, 5QI mapping, NrdcSnTermination
FR2 only activates licenses. MN is ready for NR-DC but SN's underlying parameters are hostile to SCG stability
NR-DC setup SUCCEEDS — Xn works, XnAP SCG Addition completes, rrcReconfiguration sent to UE
SCG collapses within milliseconds — SN-side parameters (t310, CG switch, no rsrpCritical) cause immediate failure
Abnormal release rate spikes — all SCG releases are abrupt because proactive PSCell management is disabled

cross_domain_chains.systemic_scope: Every venue where FR1+FR2 scripts are deployed without SN baseline tuning will exhibit this pattern
cross_domain_chains.resolution: Enhance FR2 script with SN-side parameter configuration per PB_FR2_SCRIPT_ENHANCEMENT

cross_domain_chains.name: Multi-gNB Venue Architecture Chain
cross_domain_chains.description: Causal chain from venue physical architecture through parameter coordination to KPI impact.

steps.description: 4 gNBs deployed at Angel Stadium with 7 NR nodes
Sequential PCI blocks allocated (882-902) to prevent collision
Shared TAC 3238400 eliminates intra-venue TAU
CORENET VLANs 2267/2313 isolate user plane traffic
16 AMFs per gNB across 6 regional pools for failover
Root sequence separation prevents cross-sector RACH collision
Aggregate venue RACH SR maintained above 99%
95-cell venue maintains 95%+ availability with multi-gNB redundancy
